channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: You can get paid $2,500 to shop at Costco for a week
item.description: Attention Costco shoppers! How would you like to get paid $2,500 plus a free Costco membership to shop at your favorite store?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KREX 5

item.title: Chuck Woolery, former host of 'Wheel of Fortune' and more, dies
item.description: Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Winter storms possible across US during Thanksgiving week, forecasts warn
item.description: Forecasters throughout the U.S. issued warnings that another round of winter weather could complicate travel leading up to the Thanksgiving holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Earth bids farewell to its temporary 'mini moon' that is possibly a chunk of our actual moon
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Planet Earth is parting company with an asteroid that’s been tagging along as a “mini moon” for the past two months. The harmless space rock will peel away on Monday, overcome by the stronger tug of the sun’s gravity. But it will zip closer for a quick visit in January.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: After Trump's win, Black women are rethinking their role as America's reliable political organizers
item.description: Donald Trump's victory has dismayed many politically engaged Black women, and they're reassessing their enthusiasm for politics and organizing.
item.dc:creator: Kenya Hunter, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Moscow offers debt forgiveness to new recruits and AP sees wreckage of a new Russian missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a law granting debt forgiveness to new army recruits who enlist to fight in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Maryland man found guilty of killing girlfriend, unborn baby in county's first-ever conviction of its kind
item.description: A Maryland man was found guilty in the murder of his girlfriend and her unborn baby, the first time the county convicted a defendant of killing an unborn child.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chuck Woolery, smooth-talking game show host of 'Love Connection' and 'Scrabble,' dies at 83
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Will a winter storm hit the US over Thanksgiving week? Here's what forecasts show so far
item.description: WINDSOR, Calif. (AP) — Forecasters warned over the weekend that another round of winter weather could complicate travel leading up to Thanksgiving in parts of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GODOFREDO A. VASQUEZ and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fred Harris, former US senator from Oklahoma and presidential hopeful, dies at 94
item.description: Fred Harris, a former U.S. senator from Oklahoma, presidential hopeful and populist who championed Democratic Party reforms in the turbulent 1960s, died Saturday. He was 94. Harris’ wife, Margaret Elliston, confirmed his death to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A man called 911 for help during a home invasion. Las Vegas police fatally shot him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A Las Vegas man called for police help during a home invasion before an officer fatally shot him, according to authorities and 911 calls. Brandon Durham called 911 early Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO head and Trump meet in Florida for talks on global security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump and the head of NATO have met for talks on global security, the military alliance said Saturday. In a brief statement, NATO said Trump and its secretary general, Mark Rutte, met on Friday in Palm Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: New floats you'll see in Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: Six new balloons and seven new floats will appear in this year's Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade through New York City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Mocker, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — The owners of a Colorado funeral home who let nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and gave grieving families fake ashes pleaded guilty on Friday to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bodycam shows shooting of teenage murder suspect in northeast Columbus
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) – Columbus police have released footage of a shooting in which officers shot a teenager wanted for murder. Sgt.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: John Hopkins surgeon and COVID contrarian Marty Makary selected to lead Trump’s FDA
item.description: President-elect Trump has picked Marty Makary, a Johns Hopkins's surgeon who has espoused contrarian views about the COVID-19 pandemic, to lead the Federal Drug Administration. Makary is a surgical oncologist at Johns Hopkins University and an author.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Conor McGregor must pay $250K to woman who says he raped her, civil jury rules
item.description: A woman who claimed mixed martial arts fighter Conor McGregor “brutally raped and battered” her in a Dublin hotel penthouse was awarded nearly 250,000 Euros ($257,000) on Friday by a civil court jury in Ireland.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas education board approves optional Bible-infused curriculum for elementary schools
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas’ education board voted Friday to allow Bible-infused teachings in elementary schools under optional new curriculum that could test boundaries between religion and public classrooms in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: American Airlines expands software to improve boarding process
item.description: The software will not allow a boarding pass to be scanned if someone tries to board ahead of their group.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, NewsNation

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family of woman who went missing on cross-country vacation says cryptic texts sent from phone unlike her
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi never got on her flight from LA to NYC on Nov. 8 and disappeared in Los Angeles; new surveillance footage shows her with a mysterious man, her family says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attack of the trees: Aging giants wreak havoc in Missouri neighborhood
item.description: "We’re pretty concerned. We’re thinking, ‘Should we move before the trees destroy our home?’” said one resident.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man arrested, charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange: FBI
item.description: According to the FBI, a Florida man has been arrested and charged in a plot to bomb the New York Stock Exchange this week.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge dismisses wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against Moab, Utah police
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — A Utah judge on Wednesday dismissed a wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against the city of Moab alleging police did not do enough during a traffic stop to protect their daughter from the man who killed her weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donald Trump to nominate Dr. Oz for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to nominate former Pennsylvania Senate nominee Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Administrator.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5
